   "source": [
    "# Field API: ScalarField Basics\n",
    "\n",
    "[![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/tatsuki-washimi/gwexpy/blob/main/docs/web/en/user_guide/tutorials/field_scalar_intro.ipynb)\n",
    "\n",
    "This notebook teaches the basic usage of the `ScalarField` class in `gwexpy`.\n",
    "\n",
    "## What is ScalarField?\n",
    "\n",
    "`ScalarField` is a specialized class for handling 4-dimensional data consisting of time and 3D space. It represents the spacetime structure of physical fields and provides the following features:\n",
    "\n",
    "- **Axis 0 (time axis)**: Time domain ↔ Frequency domain transformations\n",
    "- **Axes 1-3 (spatial axes)**: Real space ↔ K-space (wavenumber space) transformations\n",
    "- **4D structure preservation**: Always maintains 4 dimensions even when sliced\n",
    "- **Batch operations**: Bulk processing of multiple fields using `FieldList` and `FieldDict`\n",
    "- **Signal processing**: PSD calculation, frequency-space mapping, cross-correlation, coherence maps\n",
    "\n",
    "## Related API and theory\n",
    "\n",
    "- **Related API**: [Fields API](../../reference/api/fields.rst), [ScalarField guide](../../reference/ScalarField.md)\n",
    "- **Related theory**: [Architecture and Data Flow](../architecture.md), [Validated Algorithms](../validated_algorithms.md)"
   ]
  },
  {

   "source": [
    "# Suppress SWIGLAL and other warnings for clean output\n",
    "import warnings\n",
    "\n",
    "warnings.filterwarnings(\"ignore\", \"Wswiglal-redir-stdio\")\n",
    "warnings.filterwarnings(\"ignore\", category=UserWarning)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Helper functions for visualization\n",
    "def show_field_info(field, title=\"Field Information\"):\n",
    "    \"\"\"Display field metadata as a formatted table\"\"\"\n",
    "    import pandas as pd\n",
    "    from IPython.display import HTML, display\n",
    "\n",
    "    info = {\n",
    "        'Property': ['Shape', 'Time Domain', 'Space Domains', 'Axis 0', 'Axis 1', 'Axis 2', 'Axis 3'],\n",
    "        'Value': [\n",
    "            str(field.shape),\n",
    "            field.time_domain,\n",
    "            str(field.space_domains),\n",
    "            f\"{field.axis0_name} ({field.shape[0]} points)\",\n",
    "            f\"{field.axis1_name} ({field.shape[1]} points)\",\n",
    "            f\"{field.axis2_name} ({field.shape[2]} points)\",\n",
    "            f\"{field.axis3_name} ({field.shape[3]} points)\"\n",
    "        ]\n",
    "    }\n",
    "    df = pd.DataFrame(info)\n",
    "    display(HTML(f\"<h4>{title}</h4>\"))\n",
    "    display(df.to_html(index=False, border=1))\n",
    "\n",
    "\n",
    "def compare_fields(field1, field2, name1=\"Original\", name2=\"Processed\"):\n",
    "    \"\"\"Compare two fields side by side\"\"\"\n",
    "    import pandas as pd\n",
    "    from IPython.display import HTML, display\n",
    "\n",
    "    comparison = {\n",
    "        'Property': ['Shape', 'Time Domain', 'Space Domains', 'Max Difference'],\n",
    "        name1: [\n",
    "            str(field1.shape),\n",
    "            field1.time_domain,\n",
    "            str(field1.space_domains),\n",
    "            'N/A'\n",
    "        ],\n",
    "        name2: [\n",
    "            str(field2.shape),\n",
    "            field2.time_domain,\n",
    "            str(field2.space_domains),\n",
    "            f\"{np.max(np.abs(field1.value - field2.value)):.2e}\"\n",
    "        ]\n",
    "    }\n",
    "    df = pd.DataFrame(comparison)\n",
    "    display(HTML(\"<h4>Field Comparison</h4>\"))\n",
    "    display(df.to_html(index=False, border=1))\n"
   ]
  },

   "source": [
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "from astropy import units as u\n",
    "\n",
    "from gwexpy.fields import FieldDict, FieldList, ScalarField\n",
    "\n",
    "# Set seed for reproducibility\n",
    "np.random.seed(42)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "87a69c42",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.043791,
     "end_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:06.936649+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:06.892858+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 1. ScalarField Initialization and Metadata\n",
    "\n",
    "Create a `ScalarField` object and check its metadata."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 4,
   "id": "aebedf45",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-29T06:58:07.093093Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-29T06:58:07.092587Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-29T06:58:07.118171Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-29T06:58:07.111703Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.068211,
     "end_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:07.120107+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:07.051896+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Shape: (10, 8, 8, 8)\n",
      "Unit: V\n",
      "Axis names: ('t', 'x', 'y', 'z')\n",
      "Axis0 domain: time\n",
      "Space domains: {'x': 'real', 'y': 'real', 'z': 'real'}\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Create 4D data (10 time points × 8×8×8 spatial grid)\n",
    "nt, nx, ny, nz = 10, 8, 8, 8\n",
    "data = np.random.randn(nt, nx, ny, nz)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Define axis coordinates\n",
    "t = np.arange(nt) * 0.1 * u.s\n",
    "x = np.arange(nx) * 0.5 * u.m\n",
    "y = np.arange(ny) * 0.5 * u.m\n",
    "z = np.arange(nz) * 0.5 * u.m\n",
    "\n",
    "# Create ScalarField object\n",
    "field = ScalarField(\n",
    "    data,\n",
    "    unit=u.V,\n",
    "    axis0=t,\n",
    "    axis1=x,\n",
    "    axis2=y,\n",
    "    axis3=z,\n",
    "    axis_names=[\"t\", \"x\", \"y\", \"z\"],\n",
    "    axis0_domain=\"time\",\n",
    "    space_domain=\"real\",\n",
    ")\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"Shape: {field.shape}\")\n",
    "print(f\"Unit: {field.unit}\")\n",
    "print(f\"Axis names: {field.axis_names}\")\n",
    "print(f\"Axis0 domain: {field.axis0_domain}\")\n",
    "print(f\"Space domains: {field.space_domains}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "cf394422",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {

     "end_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:07.224773+00:00",

    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "### Checking Metadata\n",
    "\n",
    "- `axis0_domain`: Domain of axis 0 (\"time\" or \"frequency\")\n",
    "- `space_domains`: Domain of each spatial axis (\"real\" or \"k\")\n",
    "- `axis_names`: Name of each axis\n",
    "\n",
    "These metadata are automatically updated during FFT transformations."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "41d32bba",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.044001,
     "end_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:07.400972+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:07.356971+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 2. Slicing with 4D Structure Preservation\n",
    "\n",
    "An important feature of `ScalarField` is that it **always maintains 4 dimensions even when sliced**.\n",
    "Even when using integer indices, they are automatically converted to length-1 slices.\n",
    "\n",
    "**Caution:** avoid calling `np.squeeze()` (or any equivalent shape-dropping step) unless you explicitly want to leave the Field API. Keeping singleton axes preserves axis metadata and makes later FFT or plotting steps less error-prone."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 5,
   "id": "82689ba0",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-29T06:58:07.527441Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-29T06:58:07.527153Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-29T06:58:07.537093Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-29T06:58:07.534503Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.041046,
     "end_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:07.537886+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:07.496840+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Original shape: (10, 8, 8, 8)\n",
      "Sliced shape: (1, 8, 8, 8)\n",
      "Type: <class 'gwexpy.fields.scalar.ScalarField'>\n",
      "Axis names preserved: ('t', 'x', 'y', 'z')\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Slice with integer index (would become 3D with regular ndarray)\n",
    "sliced = field[0, :, :, :]\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"Original shape: {field.shape}\")\n",
    "print(f\"Sliced shape: {sliced.shape}\")  # (1, 8, 8, 8) - maintains 4D!\n",
    "print(f\"Type: {type(sliced)}\")  # Still a ScalarField\n",
    "print(f\"Axis names preserved: {sliced.axis_names}\")"
   ]
  },
  {

     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Use integer indices on multiple axes\n",
    "sliced_multi = field[0, 1, 2, 3]\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"Multi-sliced shape: {sliced_multi.shape}\")  # (1, 1, 1, 1) - still 4D\n",
    "print(f\"Value: {sliced_multi.value}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "8fc46d51",
   "metadata": {

    },
    "tags": [

    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "This behavior maintains the consistency of ScalarField objects, and metadata (axis names and domain information) are never lost."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "88e54eb4",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.017277,
     "end_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:07.801885+00:00",

   },
   "source": [
    "## 3. Time-Frequency Transformation (FFT on Axis 0)\n",
    "\n",
    "You can transform the time axis to the frequency axis using the `fft_time()` and `ifft_time()` methods.\n",
    "The same normalization as GWpy's `TimeSeries.fft()` is adopted."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 7,
   "id": "7ce98fb2",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-29T06:58:07.934058Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-29T06:58:07.933783Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-29T06:58:07.956665Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-29T06:58:07.951175Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.075254,
     "end_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:07.965114+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:07.889860+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Time domain shape: (128, 4, 4, 4)\n",
      "Frequency domain shape: (65, 4, 4, 4)\n",
      "Axis0 domain changed: time → frequency\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Create ScalarField in time domain (sine wave)\n",
    "t_dense = np.arange(128) * 0.01 * u.s\n",
    "x_small = np.arange(4) * 1.0 * u.m\n",
    "signal_freq = 10.0  # Hz\n",
    "\n",
    "# Place a 10 Hz sine wave uniformly in space\n",
    "data_signal = np.sin(2 * np.pi * signal_freq * t_dense.value)[:, None, None, None]\n",
    "data_signal = np.tile(data_signal, (1, 4, 4, 4))\n",
    "\n",
    "field_time = ScalarField(\n",
    "    data_signal,\n",
    "    unit=u.V,\n",
    "    axis0=t_dense,\n",
    "    axis1=x_small,\n",
    "    axis2=x_small.copy(),\n",
    "    axis3=x_small.copy(),\n",
    "    axis_names=[\"t\", \"x\", \"y\", \"z\"],\n",
    "    axis0_domain=\"time\",\n",
    "    space_domain=\"real\",\n",
    ")\n",
    "\n",
    "# Execute FFT\n",
    "field_freq = field_time.fft_time()\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"Time domain shape: {field_time.shape}\")\n",
    "print(f\"Frequency domain shape: {field_freq.shape}\")\n",
    "print(f\"Axis0 domain changed: {field_time.axis0_domain} → {field_freq.axis0_domain}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 8,
   "id": "1a054764",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-29T06:58:08.048090Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-29T06:58:08.047804Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-29T06:58:08.797822Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-29T06:58:08.795035Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.802658,
     "end_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:08.804256+00:00",

     "start_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:08.001598+00:00",
     "status": "completed"

   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1000x400 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    },
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Peak frequency: 10.16 Hz (expected: 10.0 Hz)\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Plot frequency spectrum (select one point in x,y,z)\n",
    "# Note: Since 4D structure is maintained even after slicing, squeeze() is used to reduce dimensions\n",
    "spectrum = np.abs(field_freq[:, 0, 0, 0].value).squeeze()\n",
    "freqs = field_freq._axis0_index.value\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.figure(figsize=(10, 4))\n",
    "plt.plot(freqs, spectrum, \"b-\", linewidth=2)\n",
    "plt.axvline(\n",
    "    signal_freq, color=\"r\", linestyle=\"--\", label=f\"{signal_freq} Hz (Input signal)\"\n",
    ")\n",
    "plt.xlabel(\"Frequency [Hz]\")\n",
    "plt.ylabel(\"Amplitude [V]\")\n",
    "plt.title(\"FFT Spectrum (ScalarField)\")\n",
    "plt.xlim(0, 50)\n",
    "plt.legend()\n",
    "plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()\n",
    "plt.show()\n",
    "\n",
    "# Check peak frequency\n",
    "peak_idx = np.argmax(spectrum)\n",
    "peak_freq = freqs[peak_idx]\n",
    "print(f\"Peak frequency: {peak_freq:.2f} Hz (expected: {signal_freq} Hz)\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "b315afb0",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.04951,
     "end_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:08.903080+00:00",

    "tags": [

   },
   "source": [
    "### Inverse FFT (Frequency → Time)\n",
    "\n",
    "You can return to the original time domain with `ifft_time()`."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 9,
   "id": "3b0db4b4",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-29T06:58:09.088817Z",

    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1000x400 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    },
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Max reconstruction error: 4.44e-16 V\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Inverse FFT\n",
    "field_reconstructed = field_freq.ifft_time()\n",
    "\n",
    "# Compare with original signal\n",
    "# Note: squeeze() to 1D to maintain 4D structure\n",
    "original = field_time[:, 0, 0, 0].value.squeeze()\n",
    "reconstructed = field_reconstructed[:, 0, 0, 0].value.squeeze()\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.figure(figsize=(10, 4))\n",
    "plt.plot(t_dense.value, original, \"b-\", label=\"Original\", alpha=0.7)\n",
    "plt.plot(t_dense.value, reconstructed.real, \"r--\", label=\"Reconstructed\", alpha=0.7)\n",
    "plt.xlabel(\"Time [s]\")\n",
    "plt.ylabel(\"Amplitude [V]\")\n",
    "plt.title(\"IFFT: Frequency → Time\")\n",
    "plt.legend()\n",
    "plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()\n",
    "plt.show()\n",
    "\n",
    "# Check error\n",
    "error = np.abs(original - reconstructed.real)\n",
    "print(f\"Max reconstruction error: {np.max(error):.2e} V\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "e5598f82",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.035233,
     "end_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:09.996599+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:09.961366+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 4. Real Space-K Space Transformation (FFT on Spatial Axes)\n",
    "\n",
    "You can transform spatial axes to wavenumber space (K-space) using `fft_space()` and `ifft_space()`.\n",
    "Signed FFT with angular wavenumber k = 2π / λ is used."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 10,
   "id": "a656f534",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-29T06:58:10.105037Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-29T06:58:10.104788Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-29T06:58:10.138561Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-29T06:58:10.132409Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.074968,
     "end_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:10.142005+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:10.067037+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Original space domains: {'x': 'real', 'y': 'real', 'z': 'real'}\n",
      "After fft_space: {'y': 'real', 'z': 'real', 'kx': 'k'}\n",
      "Axis names: ('t', 'kx', 'y', 'z')\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Create data with periodic structure in space\n",
    "nx, ny, nz = 16, 16, 16\n",
    "x_grid = np.arange(nx) * 0.5 * u.m\n",
    "y_grid = np.arange(ny) * 0.5 * u.m\n",
    "z_grid = np.arange(nz) * 0.5 * u.m\n",
    "\n",
    "# Sine wave with wavelength 4m in X direction\n",
    "wavelength = 4.0  # m\n",
    "k_expected = 2 * np.pi / wavelength  # rad/m\n",
    "\n",
    "data_spatial = np.sin(2 * np.pi * x_grid.value / wavelength)[None, :, None, None]\n",
    "data_spatial = np.tile(data_spatial, (4, 1, ny, nz))\n",
    "\n",
    "field_real = ScalarField(\n",
    "    data_spatial,\n",
    "    unit=u.V,\n",
    "    axis0=np.arange(4) * 0.1 * u.s,\n",
    "    axis1=x_grid,\n",
    "    axis2=y_grid,\n",
    "    axis3=z_grid,\n",
    "    axis_names=[\"t\", \"x\", \"y\", \"z\"],\n",
    "    axis0_domain=\"time\",\n",
    "    space_domain=\"real\",\n",
    ")\n",
    "\n",
    "# FFT only on X axis\n",
    "field_kx = field_real.fft_space(axes=[\"x\"])\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"Original space domains: {field_real.space_domains}\")\n",
    "print(f\"After fft_space: {field_kx.space_domains}\")\n",
    "print(f\"Axis names: {field_kx.axis_names}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 11,
   "id": "97d751d4",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-29T06:58:10.207080Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-29T06:58:10.206815Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-29T06:58:11.054781Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-29T06:58:11.052242Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.88955,
     "end_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:11.060108+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:10.170558+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1000x400 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    },
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Peak wavenumber: 1.57 rad/m (expected: ±1.57 rad/m)\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Plot K-space spectrum\n",
    "# Note: squeeze() to reduce dimensions\n",
    "kx_spectrum = np.abs(field_kx[0, :, 0, 0].value).squeeze()\n",
    "kx_values = field_kx._axis1_index.value\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.figure(figsize=(10, 4))\n",
    "plt.plot(kx_values, kx_spectrum, \"b-\", linewidth=2)\n",
    "plt.axvline(k_expected, color=\"r\", linestyle=\"--\", label=f\"k = {k_expected:.2f} rad/m\")\n",
    "plt.axvline(-k_expected, color=\"r\", linestyle=\"--\")\n",
    "plt.xlabel(\"Wavenumber kx [rad/m]\")\n",
    "plt.ylabel(\"Amplitude\")\n",
    "plt.title(\"Spatial FFT: Real → K space\")\n",
    "plt.legend()\n",
    "plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()\n",
    "plt.show()\n",
    "\n",
    "# Check peak wavenumber\n",
    "peak_idx = np.argmax(kx_spectrum)\n",
    "peak_k = kx_values[peak_idx]\n",
    "print(f\"Peak wavenumber: {peak_k:.2f} rad/m (expected: ±{k_expected:.2f} rad/m)\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "8541fb70",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.046468,
     "end_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:11.162100+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:11.115632+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "### Wavelength Calculation\n",
    "\n",
    "In K-space, you can calculate the wavelength using the `wavelength()` method."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 12,
   "id": "22925181",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-29T06:58:11.285041Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-29T06:58:11.284782Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-29T06:58:11.311425Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-29T06:58:11.301274Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.06104,
     "end_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:11.315065+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:11.254025+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Wavelength at k=1.57: 4.00 m\n",
      "Calculated wavelengths range: 1.00 - 8.00 m\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Calculate wavelength\n",
    "wavelengths = field_kx.wavelength(\"kx\")\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"Wavelength at k={k_expected:.2f}: {2 * np.pi / k_expected:.2f} m\")\n",
    "print(\n",
    "    f\"Calculated wavelengths range: {wavelengths.value[wavelengths.value > 0].min():.2f} - {wavelengths.value[wavelengths.value < np.inf].max():.2f} m\"\n",
    ")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "eddb2101",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.029144,
     "end_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:11.370258+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:11.341114+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "### FFT on All Spatial Axes\n",
    "\n",
    "If you omit the `axes` parameter, you can FFT all spatial axes together."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 13,
   "id": "d0fd6dd7",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-29T06:58:11.520157Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-29T06:58:11.519861Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-29T06:58:11.574453Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-29T06:58:11.562444Z"
    },
    "papermill": {

     "end_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:11.576394+00:00",

     "start_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:11.490551+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "All spatial axes in K space: {'kx': 'k', 'ky': 'k', 'kz': 'k'}\n",
      "Back to real space: {'x': 'real', 'y': 'real', 'z': 'real'}\n",
      "Max reconstruction error: 2.29e-16\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# FFT on all spatial axes\n",
    "field_k_all = field_real.fft_space()\n",
    "print(f\"All spatial axes in K space: {field_k_all.space_domains}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# Return to original with inverse FFT\n",
    "field_real_back = field_k_all.ifft_space()\n",
    "print(f\"Back to real space: {field_real_back.space_domains}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# Reconstruction error\n",
    "reconstruction_error = np.max(np.abs(field_real.value - field_real_back.value))\n",
    "print(f\"Max reconstruction error: {reconstruction_error:.2e}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",

   },
   "source": [
    "## 5. Batch Operations with FieldList and FieldDict\n",
    "\n",
    "To process multiple `ScalarField` objects together, use `FieldList` or `FieldDict`."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "783045a8",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.049448,
     "end_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:11.843999+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:11.794551+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "### FieldList\n",
    "\n",
    "Manage multiple fields in list format and apply FFT operations in bulk."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 14,
   "id": "6c2d9544",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-29T06:58:11.992043Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-29T06:58:11.991786Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-29T06:58:12.019582Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-29T06:58:12.011212Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.068482,
     "end_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:12.023291+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:11.954809+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Number of fields: 3\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Create three ScalarFields with different amplitudes\n",
    "amplitudes = [1.0, 2.0, 3.0]\n",
    "fields = []\n",
    "\n",
    "for amp in amplitudes:\n",
    "    data_temp = amp * np.random.randn(8, 4, 4, 4)\n",
    "    field_temp = ScalarField(\n",
    "        data_temp,\n",
    "        unit=u.V,\n",
    "        axis0=np.arange(8) * 0.1 * u.s,\n",
    "        axis1=np.arange(4) * 0.5 * u.m,\n",
    "        axis2=np.arange(4) * 0.5 * u.m,\n",
    "        axis3=np.arange(4) * 0.5 * u.m,\n",
    "        axis_names=[\"t\", \"x\", \"y\", \"z\"],\n",
    "        axis0_domain=\"time\",\n",
    "        space_domain=\"real\",\n",
    "    )\n",
    "    fields.append(field_temp)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Create FieldList\n",
    "field_list = FieldList(fields, validate=True)\n",
    "print(f\"Number of fields: {len(field_list)}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 15,
   "id": "92419c7f",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-29T06:58:12.099089Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-29T06:58:12.098833Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-29T06:58:12.114955Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-29T06:58:12.114285Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.062265,
     "end_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:12.123200+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-29T06:58:12.060935+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "All fields transformed to frequency domain:\n",
      "  Field 0: axis0_domain = frequency\n",
      "  Field 1: axis0_domain = frequency\n",
      "  Field 2: axis0_domain = frequency\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Execute time FFT in bulk\n",
    "field_list_freq = field_list.fft_time_all()\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"All fields transformed to frequency domain:\")\n",
    "for i, field in enumerate(field_list_freq):\n",
    "    print(f\"  Field {i}: axis0_domain = {field.axis0_domain}\")"
   ]
  },

   "source": [
    "### FieldDict\n",
    "\n",
    "Manage named fields in dictionary format."
   ]
  },

   "source": [

    "    validate=True,\n",
    ")\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"Field names: {list(field_dict.keys())}\")"
   ]
  },

   "source": [
    "## 6. Signal Processing and Analysis\n",
    "\n",
    "As important signal processing techniques for physical field analysis, we demonstrate the following:\n",
    "1. **PSD calculation**: Power spectral density in time and spatial directions\n",
    "2. **Frequency-space mapping**: Visualize how frequency changes spatially\n",
    "3. **Cross-correlation**: Estimate signal propagation delays\n",
    "4. **Coherence map**: Visualize spatial coherence"
   ]
  },

   "cell_type": "code",

      "\n",

      "               [-0.04474158],\n",
      "               [-0.08665946],\n",
      "               [-0.00431457],\n",
      "               [-0.00958998]],\n",
      "\n",
      "              [[-0.12704775],\n",
      "               [-0.18034525],\n",
      "               [-0.24545939],\n",
      "               [-0.26021225],\n",
      "               [-0.27972791]],\n",
      "\n",
      "              [[-0.10539607],\n",
      "               [ 0.00241909],\n",
      "               [ 0.01135068],\n",
      "               [-0.2382848 ],\n",
      "               [-0.06614197]]]],\n",
      "            unit: V,\n",
      "            name: Environmental Field,\n",
      "            epoch: None,\n",
      "            channel: None,\n",
      "            axis_names: ['t', 'x', 'y', 'z'],\n",
      "            axis0_name: t,\n",
      "            axis1_name: x,\n",
      "            axis2_name: y,\n",
      "            axis3_name: z,\n",
      "            axis0_index: [0.00000000e+00 3.90625000e-03 7.81250000e-03 ... 3.98828125e+00\n",
      "                           3.99218750e+00 3.99609375e+00] s,\n",
      "            axis1_index: [0.         0.26315789 0.52631579 0.78947368 1.05263158\n",
      "                           1.31578947 1.57894737 1.84210526 2.10526316 2.36842105\n",
      "                           2.63157895 2.89473684 3.15789474 3.42105263 3.68421053\n",
      "                           3.94736842 4.21052632 4.47368421 4.73684211 5.        ] m,\n",
      "            axis2_index: [0.  0.5 1.  1.5 2. ] m,\n",
      "            axis3_index: [0.] m,\n",
      "            axis0_domain: time,\n",
      "            space_domains: {'x': 'real', 'y': 'real', 'z': 'real'},\n",
      "            axis0_offset: None)\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Generate simulation data\n",
    "# Signal: 30 Hz sine wave propagating from x=1m\n",
    "# Noise: Gaussian noise\n",
    "fs = 256 * u.Hz\n",
    "duration = 4 * u.s\n",
    "t = np.arange(int((fs * duration).to_value(u.dimensionless_unscaled))) / fs.value\n",
    "x = np.linspace(0, 5, 20)  # 5m, 20 points\n",
    "y = np.linspace(0, 2, 5)  # 2m, 5 points\n",
    "z = np.array([0])  # z=0\n",
    "\n",
    "shape = (len(t), len(x), len(y), len(z))\n",
    "data_sig = np.random.normal(0, 0.1, size=shape)\n",
    "\n",
    "source_freq = 30.0\n",
    "source_signal = np.sin(2 * np.pi * source_freq * t)\n",
    "v_prop = 10.0  # 10 m/s\n",
    "source_pos = 1.0\n",
    "\n",
    "for i, xi in enumerate(x):\n",
    "    dist = abs(xi - source_pos)\n",
    "    delay = dist / v_prop\n",
    "    shift = int(delay * fs.value)\n",
    "    sig_delayed = np.roll(source_signal, shift)\n",
    "    amp = 1.0 / (1.0 + dist)\n",
    "    data_sig[:, i, :, 0] += amp * sig_delayed[:, np.newaxis]\n",
    "\n",
    "field_sig = ScalarField(\n",
    "    data_sig,\n",
    "    unit=u.V,\n",
    "    axis0=t * u.s,\n",
    "    axis1=x * u.m,\n",
    "    axis2=y * u.m,\n",
    "    axis3=z * u.m,\n",
    "    name=\"Environmental Field\",\n",
    ")\n",
    "print(field_sig)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "d58a2c94",

   "source": [
    "### Power Spectral Density (PSD)\n",
    "\n",
    "Calculate the PSD at a specific location and check frequency components."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 19,
   "id": "cfb08b88",
   "metadata": {

   "source": [
    "# Compare PSD at source position (x=1m) and far field (x=4m)\n",
    "psd_source = field_sig.psd(point_or_region=(1.0 * u.m, 0.0 * u.m, 0.0 * u.m))\n",
    "psd_far = field_sig.psd(point_or_region=(4.0 * u.m, 0.0 * u.m, 0.0 * u.m))\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))\n",
    "psd_source.plot(label=\"Source (x=1m)\")\n",
    "psd_far.plot(label=\"Far (x=4m)\")\n",
    "plt.xscale(\"log\")\n",
    "plt.yscale(\"log\")\n",
    "plt.title(\"PSD at Different Locations\")\n",
    "plt.legend()\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },

    "## 6. Verification of Numerical Invariants\n",
    "\n",
    "To verify the accuracy of FFT transformations, we confirm that round-trip transformations can reconstruct the original data.\n",
    "\n",

    }

   "source": [
    "# Time FFT invariant check: ifft_time(fft_time(f)) ≈ f\n",
    "np.random.seed(42)\n",
    "data_test = np.random.randn(64, 4, 4, 4)\n",
    "field_test = ScalarField(\n",
    "    data_test,\n",
    "    unit=u.V,\n",
    "    axis0=np.arange(64) * 0.01 * u.s,\n",
    "    axis1=np.arange(4) * u.m,\n",
    "    axis2=np.arange(4) * u.m,\n",
    "    axis3=np.arange(4) * u.m,\n",
    "    axis_names=[\"t\", \"x\", \"y\", \"z\"],\n",
    "    axis0_domain=\"time\",\n",
    "    space_domain=\"real\",\n",
    ")\n",
    "\n",
    "# Round-trip\n",
    "field_roundtrip = field_test.fft_time().ifft_time()\n",
    "\n",
    "# Check\n",
    "max_error_time = np.max(np.abs(field_test.value - field_roundtrip.value.real))\n",
    "print(f\"Time FFT Round-trip max error: {max_error_time:.2e}\")\n",
    "assert max_error_time < 1e-10, \"Time FFT round-trip failed!\"\n",
    "print(\"Time FFT invariant check: PASSED\")"
   ]
  }
 ],
